item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really want to like this one, but it's too on the nose for me. A lot of it feels too forced for my liking. The concept is good, but the actual presentation of that concept is delayed until near the end of the skip. Redacting what the -A does to you feels like kind of a cop-out, if you ask me. And the last line from the host just feels too much like Laugh is Fun.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for this as it's a little old but… I need to ask a question. How would you secure, protect and contain when you don't know what something is, what it's capable of, or what it even is? As a poster said a while ago quite well, you need to break a few eggs sometimes to do something. Though in this case it's breaking reality over your knee and telling it to sit in the corner, but whatever. In-universe the perfect situation is that one or two random civilians disappear every few weeks or months.

They'll probably stop the testing once they've truly learnt absolutely everything about it. But I mean, if learning everything about it means that it could, say, spread to other forms of media (like video on the internet or a television show) if the VHS tape isn't watched in a certain period of time. That's going to cause many more casualties than maybe one or two people a month/week.
Sometimes the functional answer isn't often best, especially in the SCP universe.

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. One or more images on this article are non-compliant with that policy and have been removed. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM. Please do not readd the image until you have a source and list it in this discussion thread.

To be specific to your situation: Linking a whole website is not sufficient. You need to link to a page with both the actual images and compliant licensing information visible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think the ethics committee would ever allow testing of this when it could remove civilians from reality. It's a no from me. Remember, the foundation secures, contains, protects, not tests, tests, tests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow this is just amazing! Definitely in my top 10 favorite SCPs. Great work to the author and those who helped clean up the entry!

item.title: Re: Crit hit for +1 voting
item.content:encoded: Ah, let me clarify. I wasn't saying that Laugh isn't scary. It's just that it builds up the horror through repeatedly subverting expectations and adding layers and layers of weirdness. This article takes a different route by having less surrealism and more elements that are clearly designed to be horrifying. I do agree that more details would improve it, but I think Laugh's niche is almost filled, and making it that level of 'weird' would make it more derivative and less original. That's entirely personal preference though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Great Hippo recently posted a rather in-depth analysis of two SCPs very similar to this one. I'm not going to pretend I have that level of insight, but I think this SCP is not working in several ways to me that are relevant to that analysis.

+ show block
– hide block
  • Why the video tape?

This has the same propogation method as SCP-1981, and not for any reason that tells us about the story. 1981's tape feels like a very plausible mundane object (tape of a well-known Reagan speech) that's being warped by something within that wants to communicate with us. SCP-024 is a sounds stage that keeps popping out new game show episodes, and LAUGH IS FUN is trying its damndest to get seen on every possible platform.

The only purpose I can find for this video tape is that it's a trap for the viewer. That doesn't come out until much later in the document, and frankly it's not very threatening. Oh, I didn't watch the only copy of this weird tape. Guess I'm fine.

I mean, SCP-024 involved you just walking into a building, but even that felt more like a plausible threat. If this thing really is predatory, why one tape? If it's the single incursion from another universe, I'm into that, but it doesn't fit that structure well.

  • Really ineffective heightening

So the only motivation we get from the tape is that it attacks the viewers relatives. (Also it makes your family watch and they love it?) That's not super interesting as far as motivations go (and not super scary as I said above) and feels like a bit of a non-sequitur. After that, you have all kinds of methods for heightening the fear that really don't do much.
- It's your FAVORITE relative! Scared yet?
- It retrocausally erases them if they die! I wasn't scared about that, and this impossibility is pretty blandly stated in exposition, so it just made this less plausible. (Compare that to LAUGH IS FUN's abductions/body disappearances, which is possible and fucking weird, or the retrocausal fun-and-games of something like SCP-1310 or SCP-2140. This is just… there.)
- You keep telling us how we or they feel. You as a viewer are distressed and panicked, the contestants are distressed and panicked. Again, plain exposition for emotional human reactions, not super effective. The exception to this when the survivors refuse to watch the tape, which was cool.

  • "appears to be the capture and [REDACTED]"

This is super-tropey and boring redaction. If it's unknown because it's off-camera and the Foundation don't know what it is like, as in the later log, that might cause me to think about it. [Graphic violence redacted, you think of it] just feels lazy. Like, "beats them to death with its bare hands", the simplest answer possible, would be vastly more interesting.

  • "Research personnel have speculated SCP-3139-1 is subject to the laws of time and exists as a separate reality from our own."

This feels like a leap.

  • Kinda boring game show

The only things going on in the game show are 1) it has kinda a boring name, 2) the name implies a maze although we have no detail, 3) I guess you can win, and 4) you're chased and killed by -A. All the other weirdness comes from the viewer/relative/audience stuff. I'm not saying the game show needs to be crazier, but those other elements don't feel very thematically related or scary, and in the case of the audience just didn't make much sense. The infertility thing, for instance, felt like a potentially decent source of horror but kinda tacked on like many other effects.

THINGS THAT DID WORK
- The dialog of the host and stuff with Chomper are pretty creepy. I like that the survivors won't talk about it, I like that Chomper is aging and craves recognition from these horrible people. As much as I criticized the game show above, it is pretty spooky in the end.

Edit: one more thought on retrocausality:

+ show block
– hide block

The reason I really like the relatives refusing to watch is that it gives conflicting evidence.
There's no evidence of the company or set existing. The relatives don't know what you're talking about.
Oh, maybe it isn't real.
They vehemently refuse to watch the video.
Oh shit.

Compare this with the retrocausal effect. The Foundation knows for a fact that this erases relatives, and thus so do we. There's no question as to whether this tape has a direct influence on reality, it definitely does. There's no mystery or fun. There's no question of whether the viewers are losing their minds and imagining relatives disappearing. This is where other retrocausal SCPs shine, and where this one does nothing.

Sure, the it makes sense that the Foundation would've put the pieces together eventually. This isn't their first retrocausal rodeo. But there might have been a time when they hadn't. You've chosen to see this in the less interesting time.

Ok, having rambled through all of that, parting thoughts. This feels like you have several threads going:
- Spooky video tape that targets the viewer
- Crazy relative-based horror
- Creepy murderous game show
I think that the video tape idea is currently the weakest and is sapping horror from the relative stuff, which is also kinda confusing and not well integrated. The game show elements are creepy, even if the base idea is pretty generic. It has elements that are unsettling, but it also has a lot of elements that aren't accomplishing much and never quite gel.

-1 I guess. Edit: I don't hate this, it's just in a crowded genre where it doesn't stand out, and could probably be exceptional with a bit more development.

item.title: Re: Crit hit for +1 voting
item.content:encoded:
This is reminiscent of Laugh Is Fun, but less surreal and more thought provoking. It doesn't have the wacky charm of Laugh, but it plays on the same tropes in a different way, taking a straight path to horror instead of meandering weirdness.

I don't agree, before I read The More You Know, Laugh Is Fun was the scariest article to me. The fact that it is so odd and crazy surreal is what made it so deeply uncomfortable and disturbing. I feel that this SCP could have been more effective had it been weirder and more surreal (not to say it doesn't work as is, because it does). Some more disturbing descriptions of the labyrinth and weirder behaviors by Chomper and the host would have been highly appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I made some small edits to format errors (as I perceived them). I'll list them here so you can change them back if they were intentional:

  • Changed "Object Class: Euclid" to "Object Class: Euclid"
  • Changed "[…] SCP-3139-1-A never exhibits any obvious reaction or emotion to the events of SCP-3139-1-A […] " to "[…] SCP-3139-1-A never exhibits any obvious reaction or emotion to the events of SCP-3139-1 […]"
  • Changed "[…] number one game show, 'America's Labyrinth!" to "[…] number one game show, 'America's Labyrinth!'" (added the end of some quotation marks that were left open).

Anyways, I liked it just enough for an upvote. It's not really anything I haven't seen before, but it had some details that I enjoyed quite a bit (like how Chomper ages and how the audience members react to their relatives), so I ultimately liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has all the hallmarks of something that's suitably creepy and massively appealing, but it feels like it's reaching in places; it seems like it's trying way too hard, which isn't very impressive.

nitpicks

+ show block
– hide block

The original tape on which SCP-3139-1 is stored can be found in Containment Locker 017 on the uppermost floor of Site-882.

This is oddly clunky when 'the original tape…' already has an SCP designation.

unnamed "host"

Not sure why this needed quotemarks here or anywhere else. Ditto for "studio" later on.

Multiple individuals are heard laughing throughout the tape, suggesting it was either filmed in front of or presented to a live audience.

Yet, it goes on to repeatedly reference the audience - it'd probably be less obtuse if the description were better structured.

The structure depicted in the footage has yet to be located by the Foundation and is speculated to be a construct of the anomaly itself.

Based off what, exactly? It's just thrown out there with no foundation. This is where it begins to tip from 'show' into 'tell'.

Quick aside, 3139—1-A is easily the most interesting aspect of this article.

Viewer's biological child (before and after their birth)

Aside from being an odd way to phrase this ( "Viewer's biological child (including fetuses)" may flow better.), this seems like it's trying too hard (the fetal victims). If this were something subtle weaved in for fridge horror, it might have been more satisfying.

Plus, their inclusion throws several spanners in how this works: How would the host/audience refer to someone who has yet to be named? How would fetuses or infants, even being scaled up to adults, possess the mental capacity or physical dexterity to attempt escape from a maze, or even operate their new bodies?

Should SCP-3139-1-B escape the structure (and subsequently the show), no further anomalous effects manifest.

Because it's not commented upon, am I to assume people are de-aged? Or do full-grown adults suddenly explode out of pregnant women?

Should SCP-3139-1-B be captured by SCP-3139-1-A, they will cease to exist in reality.

they will cease to exist in reality. Save for the viewer,

Conveniently, Foundation personnel are unaffected by this, because then how else can they provide exposition. More magic knowledge that's making the concept seem overwritten.

Some viewers have reported SCP-3139-1-B attempting to communicate with them, though this is inaudible over the music and sound effects of SCP-3139-1.

Neat detail that's stale after so much dry clinical prose. Had this [article as a whole] been condensed into something punchier, or if this were presented in another way, or if there was something earlier to build atmosphere, this may have been more striking, but I was already getting bored by this point.

possessing general physical similarities, suggesting they are unborn children

I'm not sure what this is supposed to mean.

In these instances, the viewer's attempts to bear children (if female) or fertilize female eggs (if male) will always fail despite showing no signs of clinical sterility. No known methods to counteract this are currently known.

Why? This just seems random.

Video starts off fine, but I didn't really get anything out of it. Seemed over-and-done without doing much in the way to build suspense or add atmosphere. The final line is what really elicited a groan:

We claim another. We claim another. Glorious prey. Your [inaudible] are delicious. Delicious.

Which seemed narmy, generic, and extremely unsatisfying.

TLDR: There are far too many issues with this for me to get invested. Between contrived exposition, needless complexity, poorly-structured information, and try-hard spoopiness, I've got no choice but to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They have to test everything, just to be safe - sure, a few random civilians disappear forever. But what if, for example, the "audience" grew exponentially while the tape sat in containment because being watched every now and then had been slowing it down? If they don't experiment to find the parameters of the anomaly, they could miss something like that. They can't afford to take the chance; their mission won't allow them to.

Sometimes, to make an omelette, you have to break some eggs. Sometimes the eggs are people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a no vote for me. Given that this SCP targets family members and not the viewers themselves, why would they ever test it? Even on D-Class, the loss of some random non-D class citizen should be unacceptable for the Foundation. If there was some addendum that testing was done before full knowledge of its effects, that would be fine.

Given the containment procedures, it seems that they are okay with testing this even further. It represents a danger to your average person and is potentially feeding this entity. Doesn't make sense. It's creepy for sure, but in universe doesn't make much sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I really enjoyed the successive iterations making things more and more personal.

item.title: Crit hit for +1 voting
item.content:encoded:
Alright, that title was dumb. Apologies.

+ Show Critique
- Hide Critique

manufactured by "KIPPER MEDIA, INC." sometime in the 1980s.

"sometime" sounds very slightly informal here. But hey, that's just personal preference.

SCP-3139-1-A will stalk and pursue the camera operator

How can you stalk someone without pursuing them? I get what you mean, but it seems a little redundant. "Stalk", also sounds a tad to un-clinical for an SCP document.

Object Class: Euclid

Un-bolden that "Euclid" soldier!

(see Addendum II for further information on the latter).

The redaction almost turned me off the article, but the fact that you clarified it shows a fairly good grasp of how people on the site think :P
"Further information" is inaccurate though — you don't give any information to begin with.

SCP-3139-1 is subject to the laws of time and exists as a separate reality from our own.

But -1 is the contents of a tape. Do you mean something along the lines of 'the area depicted in SCP-3139-1'?

Viewer's biological child (before and after their birth)

Something about this sentence gets me in all the wrong ways. Well done.

our lucky contestant was…less than lucky

Space after ellipse.

Host: We claim another. We claim another. Glorious prey. Your [inaudible] are delicious. Delicious. [audience laughter]

Oh my god.

This is reminiscent of Laugh Is Fun, but less surreal and more thought provoking. It doesn't have the wacky charm of Laugh, but it plays on the same tropes in a different way, taking a straight path to horror instead of meandering weirdness. I felt the recording could have gone on longer and provided a little more in the way of atmosphere, but the real-world consequences combined with the effective imagery comes together to make this surprisingly horrifying for a people-stealing game show. You've clearly put effort in with regards to pacing and structure, and it's payed off in spades.

Easy +1, and I hope to see more of this stuff in the future :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1.

The only thing I'd recommend changing right now is a small bit in the initial description. The tape is described as being made "sometime in the 1980s." I don't think that fits with the tone very well, and it broke immersion a bit on the first read. You could probably change it to "manufactured in 198█" and it'd work.

Other than that, very nicely done. Everything builds on previous information to create something thoroughly unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you think they air Bobble during the day?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So i'm assuming this is an infovore like Alistair Grey?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta say I'm most curious as to what the prizes are.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Just a question about the images, how does this site work? Do you buy images or something? Is this allowed by the sites image rules?

Sorry if I’m getting this site wrong.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: SCP-XXXX-1-B is present in the recording, level 3. Did you get that yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can see this playing on the same network as L AU GH. It's got a good creepypasta feel. Definitely would not reread in a dark room.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
What edit are you proposing?

Thanks for the compliment

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Could us a slight edit in the transcript.

Gave me chills.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Special thanks to everyone in #site19 that gave this thing feedback.

All images taken from http://www.videoblocks.com/
